channel.title: Dev Jurnal
channel.description: A developer blog on software development, engineering practices, and lessons learned from building real software.

item.title: Choosing the Right Web Framework: A Practical Guide
item.description: How to choose a web framework by evaluating learning curve, community, performance, and fit — without falling into analysis paralysis.

item.title: Duplication vs Wrong Abstraction
item.description: Why duplicating code is often safer than premature abstraction, and how to discover the right abstractions as real patterns emerge over time.

item.title: Trade-off: Best Practice Cost vs ROI
item.description: Lessons learned about how and when we need best practice

item.title: Lessons from a Global Hackathon: Building an AI for Certificate Extraction
item.description: Lessons from my first global hackathon: building an AI certificate extraction tool with AWS Lambda, S3, and Claude Sonnet 3.5 via Bedrock.

item.title: Why Most Internal Tools Fail (and How to Build One That Doesn’t)
item.description: Lessons learned from building custom software for real operational problems

item.title: It’s Not Just the Model. It’s the Combination
item.description: Comparing AI coding tools isn't just about the model — the agent matters too. My experience testing Copilot, Claude, and Zed with different model backends.

item.title: First Job Experience as a Web Developer
item.description: Reflecting on my first year as a web developer: owning projects solo in a three-person team and the lessons that still shape how I work today.

item.title: If Something Feels Too Simple, You Probably Haven’t Seen the Details
item.description: Why systems that feel simple are usually just revealing their surface — a reflection on the gap between using an API and truly understanding the system.

item.title: Trying to Apply the Laws of TDD
item.description: After actually practicing TDD's three laws, I discovered benefits that reading about it never made obvious — including reduced cognitive load and guided momentum.

item.title: How I Use Upwork Jobs as My Learning Roadmap
item.description: ‘Instead of chasing tutorials, I browse Upwork job posts to discover what skills the market actually wants — then learn those. Here is how I use job listings as a practical, demand-driven learning curriculum.’

item.title: Implementing Static Search in Astro Using Pagefind
item.description: Learn how to implement a fast, static search feature in Astro using Pagefind—no custom scripts or heavy JavaScript required.
